steps.title: Identify the narrator's central activity
steps.content: The strongest signal is the “measuring eyes” gazing upon “two separate worlds,” combined with her constant effort to “explain...all the queer things” she encounters. This captures her active observation and sense-making.

steps.title: Match the purpose to the answer
steps.content:
Choice A captures the core purpose: the narrator is “observing and making sense of differences between two cultures she embraces” — exactly what the passage shows.

The others miss this focus by emphasizing forming connections (B), suggesting hesitation (C), or misinterpreting the metaphor as literal distance (D).

That matches choice A — A is correct.
